label: Reinforcement learning

node.label: Reinforcement learning
node.definition: An agent learns behavior from rewards while interacting with an environment over time.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Reinforcement learning is to connect Game theory & decision theory, Dynamical systems & control theory to downstream capabilities including Model-based reinforcement learning, Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives.
examples: substrate for Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning
substrate for Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Game theory & decision theory, Dynamical systems & control theory.

dependencies.rationale: Reinforcement learning uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Reinforcement learning uses Dynamical systems & control theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Reinforcement learning uses Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Reinforcement learning uses Labels, targets, rewards & preferences as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Reinforcement learning uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.

sentence_units.text: An agent learns behavior from rewards while interacting with an environment over time.

The role of Reinforcement learning is to connect Game theory & decision theory, Dynamical systems & control theory to downstream capabilities including Model-based reinforcement learning, Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives.
